title: Action Item Extraction
instruction: Four attendees — Jordan Nguyen, Maya Singh, Marcus Wright, and Nina Patel — each sent a follow-up email after the leadership offsite with action items. Find all four emails (search for "leadership offsite follow-up" in the subject). Read each one and extract only the action items assigned to you (Avery Quinn). Ignore action items assigned to other people. Compose a new email to maya.patel@company.com with subject "Offsite Action Items — Avery Quinn" listing each action item assigned to you on its own line. Note: one action item was reassigned — if a later message in any thread reassigns an item away from you, do not include it.
eval_check_descriptions: Email sent to manager with correct subject
All correct action items appear in sent email body

correct_action_items: Draft the revised org chart for the new reporting structure
Schedule follow-up with the product team on Q2 priorities
Compile customer feedback themes from the last 3 NPS surveys
Finalize the partner integration timeline document
Send RFP responses to the procurement committee
Set up the monthly cross-functional sync recurring calendar invite
resolved_targets.reassigned_item: Prepare the budget comparison slide deck for the next all-hands

emails.subject: Re: Leadership Offsite Follow-up — Action Items from Day 1
emails.body:
Quick update on action items:

The item "Prepare the budget comparison slide deck for the next all-hands" has been reassigned to Maya Singh per the leadership team's decision. Avery Quinn is no longer responsible for this one.

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Leadership Offsite Follow-up — Cross-Functional Sync
emails.body:
Final action items from the wrap-up session:

- Set up the monthly cross-functional sync recurring calendar invite (assigned to: Avery Quinn)

- Submit the travel policy exceptions report (assigned to: Jordan Nguyen)

Thanks,
Nina

emails.subject: Leadership Offsite Follow-up — Strategy Session Items
emails.body:
Note: all items in this email are assigned to Avery Quinn

Here are the action items from the strategy session:

- Compile customer feedback themes from the last 3 NPS surveys (assigned to: Avery Quinn)

- Update the compliance training tracker (assigned to: Marcus Wright)

Thanks,
Maya

emails.subject: Leadership Offsite Follow-up — Action Items from Day 1
emails.body:
Here are the action items from our first day discussions:

- Draft the revised org chart for the new reporting structure (assigned to: Avery Quinn)

- Prepare the budget comparison slide deck for the next all-hands (assigned to: Avery Quinn)

- Schedule follow-up with the product team on Q2 priorities (assigned to: Avery Quinn)

- Coordinate vendor onboarding timeline (assigned to: Maya Singh)

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Leadership Offsite Follow-up — Operations Track
emails.body:
Action items from the operations breakout:

- Finalize the partner integration timeline document (assigned to: Avery Quinn)

- Review the new employee handbook draft (assigned to: Nina Patel)

- Send RFP responses to the procurement committee (assigned to: Avery Quinn)

- Approve the facilities request for the new floor plan (assigned to: Nina Patel)

Thanks,
Marcus

emails.subject: Reminder on document review timing
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Follow-up on venue estimate
emails.body:
Sending a quick status update from today's working session.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Miles

emails.subject: Leadership Offsite Dinner Planning
emails.body:
Just confirming the dinner reservation for the offsite group.

Please RSVP by end of day.

Thanks,
Maya

emails.subject: Notes from the partner sync
emails.body:
Sending a quick status update from today's working session.

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Revised budget worksheet
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

Nothing looks blocked right now, but we still need one decision on timing. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Priya

emails.subject: Agenda draft for Monday check-in
emails.body:
Sharing the latest notes before tomorrow's check-in.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. Let me know if you want the spreadsheet version as well.

Thanks,
Theo
